channel.title: picks on Ekansh Goenka
channel.description: Recent content in picks on Ekansh Goenka

item.title: Clean Code, Horrible Performance
item.description: Just proves and benchmarks how slow clean code really is and how pointless and bad it can be sometimes. Just an absolutely great article and video and it’s brilliant. I absolutely love it. Casey Muratori is a great guy and super talented engineer.

item.title: Code: The Hidden Language of Computer Hardware and Software
item.description: This book helped me visualize how computers work from the bottom up. I couldn’t stop reading. I finished the whole book in almost three days. It’s so interesting, super well written, and just absolutely a great read. This changed my perspective on CS forever.

item.title: Linux
item.description: A brilliant open-source operating system. Lightweight, performant, transparent, and really well-made overall. I love it for desktop use, server use, and especially embedded systems. Using Linux feels like holding a nicely crafted glass cube, whereas Windows feels like some brown plastic. At least Mac is Unix-based.

item.title: Neovim
item.description: My editor. Peak editor. Best editor (for me). Also I really like the plugins and just the whole system in general. Very nice!

item.title: Obsidian
item.description: A great free note-taking tool. I love the customizability, the graph view, and how well it supports a Zettelkasten-style approach to managing knowledge. A little bummed it’s not fully open source, but whatever. I actually built a similar alternative as a Neo4j plugin that I also use heavily.

item.title: Vim Motions
item.description: I just couldn’t live without them. They are so good, so fast, and I need them. They are perfect in every way (mostly). You don’t even need to touch the mouse. All editing happens on your nice comfy little keyboard.
